subject: grammar
title: Replacing Phrases with Adverbs
instruction: Replace the underlined part of each sentence with an adverb from the box (actually, apparently, evidently, frankly, on the whole, possibly, surprisingly, unfortunately) that has a similar meaning.
passage: Example: I've heard that he is a teacher. -> Apparently

questions.text: To be honest I don't really like this kind of literature.
I found it unexpected that the survey revealed that university students today spend longer on their work than in the past.
I'm not happy about it but I can't come to your party because I'm working.
To sum it up, it seems that people prefer to spend festivals with their families or friends rather than alone.
From what I have read in the journal, it seems that scientists believe the new drug will work.
I am not absolutely certain about this but if we reduce the amount we consume, we will become healthier.
As a matter of fact I think this is the best piece of work you've done so far.
